title: Selection
description: What conditions and selection are, and how to use if/else blocks to make a chatbot respond differently.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.css: .dn-card{display:inline-block;background:#fff;border:3px solid #cbd5e1;border-radius:10px;padding:0.75rem 1.25rem;font-size:2rem;color:#e11d48;font-weight:700;font-family:Georgia,serif;box-shadow:2px 4px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);text-align:center;min-width:90px;margin-bottom:1rem} .dn-tf-table{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;margin:0.5rem 0} .dn-tf-table th{background:#0f172a;color:#94a3b8;font-size:0.78rem;font-weight:600;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.05em;padding:0.5rem 0.75rem;text-align:left} .dn-tf-table td{padding:0.55rem 0.75rem;background:#0f172a;border-bottom:1px solid #334155;color:#e2e8f0;font-size:0.88rem;vertical-align:middle} .dn-tf-table tr:last-child td{border-bottom:none} .dn-tf-btn{border:1px solid #334155;background:#0f172a;color:#e2e8f0;border-radius:0.3rem;padding:0.25rem 0.7rem;font-size:0.8rem;cursor:pointer;font-family:inherit;margin:0 0.15rem;transition:border-color 0.1s} .dn-tf-btn:hover:not(:disabled){border-color:var(--jhncc-yellow)} .dn-tf-btn.dn-correct{background:#14251d;border-color:#4ade80;color:#dcfce7} .dn-tf-btn.dn-wrong{background:#281616;border-color:#f87171;color:#fee2e2}
steps.content:
Do Now: true or false?

Look at the card below. Decide whether each statement about it is true or false. Click your answer for each row.

7 ♥
StatementYour answer
The colour of this card is black
The suit of this card is hearts
The value of this card is greater than 5
The value of this card is exactly 10
The card is red and the value is less than 10

You just evaluated conditions - deciding whether each statement is true or false. That is exactly what a computer does when it runs a selection statement.

steps.title: What is Selection?
steps.content:
What is selection?

In the Do Now, you looked at a card and decided whether each statement was true or false. That is exactly what a computer does when it uses selection.

Key vocabulary

WordMeaning
ConditionAn expression that evaluates to either true or false
SelectionWhen a program evaluates a condition and runs different code depending on the result
if / elseThe selection statement — do one thing if the condition is true, another thing else

An everyday example:

  • if it is raining then
  • take an umbrella
  • else
  • leave the umbrella at home
  • end

Key facts

  • Every condition is either true or false — never both, never neither
  • The if branch runs when the condition is true
  • The else branch runs when the condition is false
  • If there is no else and the condition is false, the program skips to the next block

steps.title: if/else Blocks in Scratch
steps.content:
Selection blocks in Scratch

Both selection blocks are in the Control menu. The condition goes inside the diamond-shaped gap. Conditions (true/false blocks) come from the Operators and Sensing menus.

1. if only — runs the blocks inside only if the condition is true. Skips if false.

if <(answer) = [yes]> then
  say [Great!] for (2) seconds
end

2. if / else — runs the top section if true, or the bottom section if false.

if <(answer) = [yes]> then
  say [Great!] for (2) seconds
else
  say [I am sorry to hear that.] for (2) seconds
end

The equals operator from the Operators menu is used to compare the answer to a word:

<(answer) = [yes]>

A complete chatbot script using if / else:

when green flag clicked
ask [Are you ok?] and wait
if <(answer) = [yes]> then
  say [Great!] for (2) seconds
else
  say [I am sorry to hear that.] for (2) seconds
end

steps.title: Predict the Output
steps.content:
Predict the output

Read this script carefully and trace through each block from top to bottom.

when green flag clicked
ask [Are you ok?] and wait
if <(answer) = [yes]> then
  say [Great!] for (2) seconds
else
  say [I am sorry to hear that.] for (2) seconds
end

The user types no. What does the sprite say?
steps.js: createMCQ('predict-q',['Great!','I am sorry to hear that.','Are you ok?','Nothing — the if block is skipped'],1,'Correct! The condition (answer = yes) is false because the user typed "no", so the else branch runs instead.','Not quite. Trace it: answer is "no", so (answer = yes) is false — the else branch runs, not the if branch.');

steps.title: Build: Are You OK?
steps.content:
Build it: responding differently

Build a chatbot that asks if the user is ok and gives a different response depending on the answer.

  1. Add when green flag clicked (Events)
  2. Attach ask [Are you ok?] and wait (Sensing)
  3. From Control, drag an if / else block onto the script
  4. From Operators, drag the ( ) = ( ) block into the diamond gap
  5. Drag answer from Sensing into the left slot and type yes in the right slot
  6. In the if section: add say [Great!] for (2) seconds
  7. In the else section: add say [I am sorry to hear that.] for (2) seconds

Your finished script

when green flag clicked
ask [Are you ok?] and wait
if <(answer) = [yes]> then
  say [Great!] for (2) seconds
else
  say [I am sorry to hear that.] for (2) seconds
end
steps.js: (function(){var NW=1100,NH=650;function scaleFrame(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(!frame)return;var wrap=frame.parentElement;var bar=wrap.querySelector('.y7-check-bar');var barH=bar?bar.offsetHeight:44;var isFs=wrap.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen');var availW=isFs?window.innerWidth:wrap.clientWidth;var scale=isFs?Math.min(availW/NW,(window.innerHeight-barH)/NH):availW/NW;frame.style.width=NW+'px';frame.style.height=NH+'px';frame.style.transform='scale('+scale+')';frame.style.marginBottom=Math.round(NH*(scale-1))+'px';frame.style.marginLeft=isFs?Math.max(0,Math.round((availW-NW*scale)/2))+'px':'0';}function toggleFs(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');var wrap=frame.parentElement;var btn=document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn');var isFs=wrap.classList.toggle('y7-fullscreen');btn.textContent=isFs?'✕':'⛶';btn.title=isFs?'Exit full screen (Esc)':'Full screen';if(isFs){window._y7ScrollY=window.scrollY;document.body.style.overflow='hidden';}else{document.body.style.overflow='';window.scrollTo(0,window._y7ScrollY||0);}scaleFrame();}scaleFrame();if(window._y7Resize)window.removeEventListener('resize',window._y7Resize);window._y7Resize=scaleFrame;window.addEventListener('resize',window._y7Resize);if(window._y7Key)document.removeEventListener('keydown',window._y7Key);window._y7Key=function(e){if(e.key==='Escape'){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))toggleFs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._y7Key);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=toggleFs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');var result=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=frame.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Editor still loading — wait a moment and try again.';return;}var targets=vm.runtime.targets;var hasAsk=false,hasIfElse=false,hasEq=false;for(var t=0;t<targets.length;t++){var blks=targets[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in blks){var b=blks[id];if(b.opcode==='sensing_askandwait')hasAsk=true;if(b.opcode==='control_if_else')hasIfElse=true;if(b.opcode==='operator_equals')hasEq=true;}}if(hasAsk&&hasIfElse&&hasEq){result.style.color='#86efac';result.textContent='✓ Great work! Your chatbot uses if/else to respond differently.';__markStepComplete();}else{var miss=[];if(!hasAsk)miss.push('ask block');if(!hasIfElse)miss.push('if/else block');if(!hasEq)miss.push('equals operator');result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Still missing: '+miss.join(', ')+'.';};}catch(e){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Could not check: '+e.message;console.error(e);}};})();

steps.title: Build: Easter Egg
steps.content:
Build it: Easter egg

An Easter egg is a hidden feature that appears only under a specific condition. Add one to your chatbot — if the user says their name is Ed, show a special response.

  1. Go to Variables and click Make a Variable — name it name
  2. Add when green flag clicked
  3. Attach ask [What is your name?] and wait (Sensing)
  4. Attach set [name] to (answer) (Variables)
  5. From Control, drag an if / else block
  6. From Operators, drag ( ) = ( ) into the diamond — put name in the left slot and type Ed in the right slot
  7. In the if section: say [Oh wow, same as me!] for (2) seconds
  8. In the else section: say (join [Hello ] (name)) for (2) seconds

Your finished script

when green flag clicked
ask [What is your name?] and wait
set [name v] to (answer)
if <(name) = [Ed]> then
  say [Oh wow, same as me!] for (2) seconds
else
  say (join [Hello ] (name)) for (2) seconds
end

scale=isFs?Math.min(availW/NW,(window.innerHeight-barH)/NH):availW/NW;frame.style.width=NW+'px';frame.style.height=NH+'px';frame.style.transform='scale('+scale+')';frame.style.marginBottom=Math.round(NH*(scale-1))+'px';frame.style.marginLeft=isFs?Math.max(0,Math.round((availW-NW*scale)/2))+'px':'0';}function toggleFs(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');var wrap=frame.parentElement;var btn=document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn');var isFs=wrap.classList.toggle('y7-fullscreen');btn.textContent=isFs?'✕':'⛶';btn.title=isFs?'Exit full screen (Esc)':'Full screen';if(isFs){window._y7ScrollY=window.scrollY;document.body.style.overflow='hidden';}else{document.body.style.overflow='';window.scrollTo(0,window._y7ScrollY||0);}scaleFrame();}scaleFrame();if(window._y7Resize)window.removeEventListener('resize',window._y7Resize);window._y7Resize=scaleFrame;window.addEventListener('resize',window._y7Resize);if(window._y7Key)document.removeEventListener('keydown',window._y7Key);window._y7Key=function(e){if(e.key==='Escape'){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))toggleFs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._y7Key);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=toggleFs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');var result=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=frame.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Editor still loading — wait a moment and try again.';return;}var targets=vm.runtime.targets;var hasSet=false,hasEq=false,hasIf=false;for(var t=0;t<targets.length;t++){var blks=targets[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in blks){var b=blks[id];if(b.opcode==='data_setvariableto')hasSet=true;if(b.opcode==='operator_equals')hasEq=true;if(b.opcode==='control_if'||b.opcode==='control_if_else')hasIf=true;}}if(hasSet&&hasEq&&hasIf){result.style.color='#86efac';result.textContent='✓ Easter egg added! Your chatbot uses selection to give a special response.';__markStepComplete();}else{var miss=[];if(!hasSet)miss.push('set variable');if(!hasEq)miss.push('equals operator');if(!hasIf)miss.push('if or if/else block');result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Still missing: '+miss.join(', ')+'.';};}catch(e){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Could not check: '+e.message;console.error(e);}};})();

steps.title: Tracing Selection
steps.content:
Tracing selection

Tracing means following a program line by line and working out what happens. With selection, you must check the condition first before deciding which blocks run.

Trace through the script below. The user types no.

when green flag clicked
set [score v] to (0)
ask [Did you win?] and wait
if <(answer) = [yes]> then
  set [score v] to ((score) + (10))
end
say (score) for (2) seconds

What does the sprite say?
steps.js: createMCQ('trace-q',['0','10','yes','no'],0,'Correct! score starts at 0. The condition (answer = yes) is false because the user typed "no", so the if block is skipped entirely. score is still 0 when say runs.','Not quite. Trace it: score = 0, answer = "no", so (answer = yes) is false — the set inside the if block never runs. score stays 0.');

steps.title: End of Lesson Check
steps.content:
End of lesson check

Answer all four questions. The correct answer is shown after each one.
steps.js: createMCQSet('exam-q',[{q:'What is a condition?',opts:['A block that moves a sprite','An expression that evaluates to true or false','A type of loop','A variable that changes value'],correct:1,feedback:'Correct — a condition is an expression that is either true or false.',wrongFeedback:'A condition is an expression that evaluates to true or false. Selection uses conditions to decide which code to run.'},{q:'Which menu in Scratch contains the if/else block?',opts:['Sensing','Operators','Variables','Control'],correct:3,feedback:'Correct — both if and if/else blocks are in the Control menu.',wrongFeedback:'The if and if/else blocks are in the Control menu (the gold-coloured blocks).'},{q:'The condition is (answer = yes). The user typed "no". Which branch runs?',opts:['The if branch','The else branch','Both branches','Neither branch'],correct:1,feedback:'Correct — the condition is false, so the else branch runs.',wrongFeedback:'When a condition is false, the else branch runs. The if branch only runs when the condition is true.'},{q:'What is an Easter egg in a computer program?',opts:['A bug that causes the program to crash','Hidden functionality triggered by a specific condition','A variable that stores a number','A block from the Operators menu'],correct:1,feedback:'Correct — an Easter egg is a hidden feature that appears only when a specific condition is met.',wrongFeedback:'An Easter egg is hidden functionality — a special response that only appears under a certain condition, like entering a particular name.'}]);

options: A block that moves a sprite
An expression that evaluates to true or false

quizQuestions.q: What does a selection statement do?
options: Runs different code depending on whether a condition is true or false

quizQuestions.q: Which Scratch menu contains the if/else block?
If a condition is false, which branch of an if/else block runs?
Which operator checks if two values are equal in Scratch?
A chatbot asks 'Are you ok?' The condition is (answer = yes). The user types 'no'. What happens?
If there is no else and the condition is false, what does the program do?
options: It runs the if section anyway
It skips to the next block after the if

quizQuestions.q: What is an Easter egg in programming?
options: Hidden functionality triggered by a specific condition

quizQuestions.q: Which Scratch menu contains the equals operator ( ) = ( )?
What is the difference between 'if' and 'if/else'?
options: if/else also runs different code when the condition is false; if only runs code when true
if can only be used once in a script

quizQuestions.q: Which block runs different code depending on whether a condition is true or false?
Which block checks if two values are equal?
Build a script that asks the user a yes/no question. Click Check & Submit when done.
Build a script with an if block or an if/else block. Click Check & Submit when done.
Build a condition that checks if the answer is equal to a word. Click Check & Submit when done.
Build an if/else block with a say block in each branch. Click Check & Submit when done.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer:
if <condition> then
  say [Great!]
else
  say [Try again]
end

quizQuestions.q: Build a script that asks 'Are you ok?' and uses selection to respond differently depending on the answer. Click Check & Submit when done.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer:
ask [Are you ok?] and wait
if <(answer) = [yes]> then
  say [Great!]
else
  say [I am sorry]
end
